title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: ptr_app() (Level 1, covered vignette("ggpaintr-workflow")) fastest path formula running Shiny app. also owns entire page: sidebar, draw button, plot, code output. perfect teaching quick demos, wrong anything else. vignette covers two situations ptr_app() enough: Level 2 — Embed ggpaintr Shiny app. already Shiny app (dashboard, course site, client deliverable) want one tabs hold ggpaintr-driven plot. need control layout, copy, element ids; want rewrite runtime. Level 3 — Drive runtime without full app. want plot formula without launching Shiny (batch reports, test fixtures), want post-process ggplot object rendering, want generate ggplot code strings programmatically. levels built parse/compose pipeline ptr_app() uses internally. public API gives exactly extension seams ptr_app() uses — hidden state, private bridge functions. goal instead add new placeholder type (date picker, slider, colour well), skip vignette("ggpaintr-placeholder-registry"). vignette owns placeholder-registry contract end--end; one tells register placeholders embedded headless workflow .

title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: smallest embed still behaves like ptr_app(). hand ggpaintr formula ptr_server_state(), place input output widgets inside layout, wire five default binders. Six public calls work: ptr_input_ui() renders uiOutput() slot generated controls plus “Update plot” action button. ptr_output_ui() renders plotOutput(), error uiOutput(), verbatimTextOutput() generated code. ptr_server_state(formula) parses formula returns ptr_state object holding reactive values: obj, runtime, extras, var_ui_list, shared metadata slots. object pass around every binder. ptr_setup_controls() observes dynamic var controls (choices update uploads resolve) renders tabbed control panel ids$control_panel. ptr_register_draw() observes draw button updates ptr_state$runtime() latest ptr_exec() result. ptr_register_plot() / ptr_register_error() / ptr_register_code() read reactive result render . Every binder accepts ptr_state contract. None touch private state state object — can drop binder need (example, ptr_register_code() want code output), can replace implementation reads ptr_state$runtime().
code: ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Embedded ggpaintr"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       ptr_input_ui()     ),     mainPanel(       ptr_output_ui()     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(     "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) +        geom_point() +        labs(title = text)"   )    ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state)   ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state)   ptr_register_plot(output, ptr_state)   ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state)   ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: ptr_build_ids() returns validated list describing five top-level Shiny ids ggpaintr uses. Override host app already uses one default names, want ggpaintr outputs specific containers. Full signature, defaults: Every field single non-empty string; duplicate values rejected. returned object class ptr_build_ids accepted type ids = argument every Level-2 helper. Pass ids object everywhere UI server stay sync: ptr_server_state() stores ids ptr_state$ids, every helper ids = argument defaults ptr_state$ids. Passing explicitly still useful want state object drive one output location. five top-level ids configurable. Per-placeholder input ids (ones generated ptr_parse_formula()) internal var-* helper ids deterministic package-owned.
code: ptr_build_ids(   control_panel = "controlPanel",  # uiOutput that holds the tabbed controls   draw_button   = "draw",           # actionButton that triggers a redraw   plot_output   = "outputPlot",     # plotOutput widget   error_output  = "outputError",    # uiOutput for inline error messages   code_output   = "outputCode"      # verbatimTextOutput for the generated code ) ids <- ptr_build_ids(   control_panel = "builder_controls",   draw_button   = "render_plot",   plot_output   = "main_plot",   error_output  = "main_error",   code_output   = "main_code" ) ids #> $control_panel #> [1] "builder_controls" #>  #> $draw_button #> [1] "render_plot" #>  #> $plot_output #> [1] "main_plot" #>  #> $error_output #> [1] "main_error" #>  #> $code_output #> [1] "main_code" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "ptr_build_ids" "list" ui <- fluidPage(   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       ptr_input_ui(ids = ids)     ),     mainPanel(       ptr_output_ui(ids = ids)     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(     "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) + geom_point()",     ids = ids   )    ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state, ids = ids)   ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state, ids = ids)   ptr_register_plot(output, ptr_state, ids = ids)   ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state, ids = ids)   ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state, ids = ids) }

title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: best way build intuition watch one keyword receive different copy scope. example uses text keyword title argument labs(). Calling ptr_resolve_ui_text() directly shows scope chosen given (keyword, param, layer_name) triple. returned list resolved leaf fields — whatever deepest matching layer supplied, sibling fields filled less-specific scopes. merge applies ptr_setup_controls() builds UI — per placeholder formula, using placeholder’s real (keyword, param, layer_name).
code: obj <- ptr_parse_formula(   "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) +      geom_point() +      labs(title = text)" )  ui_text <- list(   defaults = list(     text = list(       label = "Enter text",       help  = "Generic help (from defaults)"     )   ),   params = list(     title = list(       text = list(         label = "Plot title",         help  = "Arg-specific help (from params$title)"       )     )   ),   layers = list(     labs = list(       text = list(         title = list(           label       = "labs(title = …) override",           help        = "Layer-specific help (from layers$labs)",           placeholder = "e.g. Weight vs MPG"         )       )     )   ) ) # Only (keyword = "text") — lands in defaults$text ptr_resolve_ui_text(   "control", keyword = "text",   ui_text = ui_text ) #> $label #> [1] "Enter text" #>  #> $help #> [1] "Generic help (from defaults)" # Add param = "title" — now params$title$text wins on label/help, # but defaults still fill in anything params did not set. ptr_resolve_ui_text(   "control", keyword = "text", param = "title",   ui_text = ui_text ) #> $label #> [1] "Plot title" #>  #> $help #> [1] "Arg-specific help (from params$title)" # Add layer_name = "labs" — layers$labs$text$title wins on every # field it supplies, params fills in anything layers did not. ptr_resolve_ui_text(   "control", keyword = "text", param = "title", layer_name = "labs",   ui_text = ui_text ) #> $label #> [1] "labs(title = …) override" #>  #> $help #> [1] "Layer-specific help (from layers$labs)" #>  #> $placeholder #> [1] "e.g. Weight vs MPG" # A different layer with the same keyword/param — no layers entry # exists, so resolution falls back to params. ptr_resolve_ui_text(   "control", keyword = "text", param = "title", layer_name = "geom_text",   ui_text = ui_text ) #> $label #> [1] "Plot title" #>  #> $help #> [1] "Arg-specific help (from params$title)"

title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: bind helpers §3 compose three primitives: ptr_parse_formula(), ptr_runtime_input_spec(), ptr_exec(). section uses primitives directly. Everything runs outside Shiny session.

title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: Use case: generate one plot per row metadata table, render PNG, attach email. Shiny, clicks. pipeline : ptr_parse_formula(formula) → ptr_obj. ptr_runtime_input_spec(ptr_obj) → data frame, one row per Shiny input app need. Build named list mirroring spec — one slot per input_id, populated value typed. ptr_exec(ptr_obj, inputs) → runtime result. ptr_extract_plot(result) → ggplot object. spec tells exactly input_ids runtime expects. role column distinguishes three kinds input: placeholder value, per-layer /checkbox, companion name field upload placeholder. p ordinary ggplot object. can hand ggsave(), knitr chunk, downstream consumer:  runtime fails (missing column, unsafe expr, etc.), result$ok FALSE, ptr_extract_plot(result) returns NULL, result$message holds error string. shape ptr_register_plot() / ptr_register_error() consume inside Shiny.
code: obj <- ptr_parse_formula(   "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) +      geom_point() +      labs(title = text)" )  spec <- ptr_runtime_input_spec(obj) spec #>              input_id           role layer_name keyword param_key  source_id #> 1          ggplot_3_2    placeholder     ggplot     var         x ggplot_3_2 #> 2          ggplot_3_3    placeholder     ggplot     var         y ggplot_3_3 #> 3              labs_2    placeholder       labs    text     title     labs_2 #> 4 geom_point_checkbox layer_checkbox geom_point    <NA>      <NA>       <NA> #> 5       labs_checkbox layer_checkbox       labs    <NA>      <NA>       <NA> inputs <- setNames(vector("list", nrow(spec)), spec$input_id)  # Every non-ggplot layer checkbox starts TRUE (= include the layer). checkbox_rows <- spec$role == "layer_checkbox" inputs[checkbox_rows] <- rep(list(TRUE), sum(checkbox_rows))  # Fill in one placeholder value per placeholder row. pick <- function(layer_name, param_key) {   spec$input_id[spec$layer_name == layer_name & spec$param_key == param_key] } inputs[[pick("ggplot", "x")]]    <- "wt" inputs[[pick("ggplot", "y")]]    <- "mpg" inputs[[pick("labs",   "title")]] <- "Weight vs MPG"  result <- ptr_exec(obj, inputs) result$ok #> [1] TRUE p <- ptr_extract_plot(result) class(p) #> [1] "ggplot2::ggplot" "ggplot"          "ggplot2::gg"     "S7_object"       #> [5] "gg" print(p)

what: 4b. Post-process the plot inside a custom renderPlot()
title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: Use case: default ptr_register_plot() renders plot -. want add theme, scale, annotation layers depend host-app state (slider, theme picker) plot rendered, want code output keep matching formula exactly. Write renderPlot() call ptr_extract_plot() ptr_state$runtime(). default ptr_register_plot() one line — anything look roughly shape. Two things keep track : ptr_state$runtime() reactive. Read inside render function; cache . ptr_extract_plot() returns NULL first tick (draw button fired) failure. Handle . Additions attached via plain + render correctly appear code pane (added outside ptr_exec()). §4c shows bring back code output.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       ptr_input_ui(),       selectInput("theme_pick", "Theme",                   choices = c("minimal", "bw", "classic"))     ),     mainPanel(       plotOutput("outputPlot"),       verbatimTextOutput("outputCode"),       uiOutput("outputError")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(     "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) + geom_point()"   )    ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state)   ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state)   ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state)   ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state)    output$outputPlot <- renderPlot({     plot_obj <- ptr_extract_plot(ptr_state$runtime())     if (is.null(plot_obj)) {       graphics::plot.new()       return(invisible(NULL))     }      chosen <- switch(input$theme_pick,       minimal = ggplot2::theme_minimal(),       bw      = ggplot2::theme_bw(),       classic = ggplot2::theme_classic()     )     plot_obj + chosen   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: 4c. Add layers from the host app with ptr_gg_extra()
title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: Use case: §4b, also want “theme” selection host app supplies round-trip generated-code output reader can reproduce exact plot see. ptr_gg_extra() captures ggplot components add outside runtime stores ptr_state$extras. default ptr_register_code() picks extras appends code text whenever runtime succeeds. ptr_gg_extra() semantics — worth memorising: Replace, append. call overwrites previously captured extras. Pass every component want single call. Suppressed failure. runtime reports ok = FALSE, code binder ignores extras stale values prior successful draw surface failed draw. Plain list return. value plain list dispatched ggplot2::ggplot_add.list, plot_obj + ptr_gg_extra(...) behaves exactly like plot_obj + list(...). wired ptr_app(). helper meaningful caller owns renderPlot({...}) block built ptr_server_state() + ptr_register_*() helpers. write code-output binder (instead using ptr_register_code()), call ptr_extract_code(ptr_state$runtime(), extras = ptr_state$extras()) — second argument exactly ptr_register_code() passes.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       ptr_input_ui(),       checkboxInput("with_smooth", "Add geom_smooth()", FALSE)     ),     mainPanel(ptr_output_ui())   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(     "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) + geom_point()"   )    ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state)   ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state)   ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state)   ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state)    output$outputPlot <- renderPlot({     plot_obj <- ptr_extract_plot(ptr_state$runtime())     if (is.null(plot_obj)) {       graphics::plot.new()       return(invisible(NULL))     }      # Pass every component you want captured in a single call.     # ptr_gg_extra() replaces, not appends, the captured list each call.     extras <- if (isTRUE(input$with_smooth)) {       ptr_gg_extra(         ptr_state,         ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 16),         ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)       )     } else {       ptr_gg_extra(ptr_state, ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 16))     }      plot_obj + extras   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: Use case: want ggplot script generated formula + table variable assignments, students-see--code flow copy-paste RMarkdown report. ptr_extract_code() returns code single string: inputs list §4a works — runtime product shape identical whether inputs come Shiny code. ptr_extract_code() accepts optional extras = list [rlang::quosures]. code: result <- ptr_exec(obj, inputs) cat(ptr_extract_code(result)) #> ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) + #>   geom_point() + #>   labs(title = pairlist("Weight vs MPG")) extras <- list(rlang::quo(ggplot2::theme_minimal())) cat(ptr_extract_code(result, extras = extras)) #> ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) + #>   geom_point() + #>   labs(title = pairlist("Weight vs MPG")) + #>   ggplot2::theme_minimal() broken_inputs <- inputs broken_inputs[[pick("ggplot", "x")]] <- "not_a_column" bad <- ptr_exec(obj, broken_inputs) bad$ok #> [1] FALSE bad$message #> [1] "Input error: Input 'ggplot_3_2' must match one available column name for layer 'ggplot'." ptr_extract_plot(bad)    # NULL because ok = FALSE #> NULL cat(ptr_extract_code(bad))  # still returns the attempted call

what: The placeholder contract at a glance
title: Custom Placeholders with the ggpaintr Registry
text: placeholder value returned ptr_define_placeholder(). bundles five hooks copy-defaults list. two hooks required: Every hook receives two shared records: meta — describes specific occurrence placeholder formula. Fields set parser: id — Shiny input id ggpaintr generated occurrence. keyword — placeholder keyword (e.g. "date"). layer_name — ggplot layer, e.g. "geom_vline". param — argument name ("xintercept"), "" positional args. index_path — integer vector pointing parsed expression tree. context — shared read-environment: context$ptr_obj — parsed ptr_obj, including placeholder_map. context$placeholders — active placeholder registry. context$ui_text — merged copy rules. context$envir — caller environment (used resolve data symbols). context$eval_env, context$var_column_map — set Shiny mode; NULL elsewhere. copy-defaults list may set four leaf fields ggpaintr understands: label, help, placeholder, empty_text. Strings support {param} layer interpolation. Use ptr_missing_expr() resolve_expr occurrence removed completed expression (rather rendered NULL).

what: A minimal custom placeholder — a date picker
title: Custom Placeholders with the ggpaintr Registry
text: complete end--end example: define date placeholder, use formula, inspect generated metadata, launch Shiny app drives . ptr_merge_placeholders() combines custom entries built-ins registry pass downstream still understands var, text, etc. Custom entries built-keyword override built-. Parse formula enriched registry inspect metadata record parser built date occurrence: fields match meta record every hook receives — id, keyword, layer_name, param, index_path. Execute plot headlessly concrete date. Prefer ptr_runtime_input_spec(obj) discovering expected input ids programmatically rather hard-coding "geom_vline_2" pattern used clarity. launch full Shiny app drives placeholder interactively, use ptr_app() (see final section every API accepts placeholders = ...):
code: sales <- data.frame(   day = as.Date("2024-01-01") + 0:4,   value = c(10, 13, 12, 16, 18) )  date_placeholder <- ptr_define_placeholder(   keyword = "date",   build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {     shiny::dateInput(id, copy$label)   },   resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(as.character(value), "")) {       return(ptr_missing_expr())     }     rlang::expr(as.Date(!!as.character(value)))   },   copy_defaults = list(label = "Choose a date for {param}") ) placeholders <- ptr_merge_placeholders(   list(date = date_placeholder) )  names(placeholders) #> [1] "var"    "text"   "num"    "expr"   "upload" "date" obj <- ptr_parse_formula(   "ggplot(data = sales, aes(x = day, y = value)) +     geom_line() +     geom_vline(xintercept = date)",   placeholders = placeholders )  obj$placeholder_map$geom_vline[["geom_vline_2"]] #> $id #> [1] "geom_vline_2" #>  #> $keyword #> [1] "date" #>  #> $layer_name #> [1] "geom_vline" #>  #> $param #> [1] "xintercept" #>  #> $index_path #> [1] 2 runtime <- ptr_exec(   obj,   list(     "geom_line_checkbox"  = TRUE,     "geom_vline_checkbox" = TRUE,     "geom_vline_2"        = as.Date("2024-01-03")   ) )  runtime$code_text #> [1] "ggplot(data = sales, aes(x = day, y = value)) +\n  geom_line() +\n  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date(\"2024-01-03\"))" ptr_app(   "ggplot(data = sales, aes(x = day, y = value)) +     geom_line() +     geom_vline(xintercept = date)",   placeholders = placeholders )

what: A more complex example — bind_ui + prepare_eval_env
title: Custom Placeholders with the ggpaintr Registry
text: example defines log_num placeholder chooses numeric column formula’s data wraps log() generated code. uses bind_ui populate selectInput data’s numeric columns, prepare_eval_env expose small safe-log helper generated call stays readable. formula using , parsed merged registry: Running formula headlessly — note .log_safe resolves prepare_eval_env() bound eval environment: Launch live app exercise bind_ui:
code: log_num <- ptr_define_placeholder(   keyword = "log_num",    # Return a container; fill it in from bind_ui once data is available.   build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {     shiny::uiOutput(paste0(id, "_container"))   },    # Populate the selectInput with numeric columns drawn from the data   # symbol referenced in the formula's ggplot() layer.   bind_ui = function(input, output, metas, context) {     eval_env <- context$eval_env     ggplot_expr <- context$ptr_obj$expr_list[["ggplot"]]     data_expr <- if (is.call(ggplot_expr)) ggplot_expr$data else NULL     numeric_cols <- character(0)     if (!is.null(eval_env) && !is.null(data_expr)) {       d <- tryCatch(eval(data_expr, envir = eval_env), error = function(e) NULL)       if (is.data.frame(d)) {         numeric_cols <- names(d)[vapply(d, is.numeric, logical(1))]       }     }     for (meta in metas) {       local({         m <- meta         output[[paste0(m$id, "_container")]] <- shiny::renderUI({           shiny::selectInput(             m$id,             label   = paste("Log-transform column for", m$param),             choices = numeric_cols           )         })       })     }     invisible(NULL)   },    # Generate `.log_safe(<col>)` in the completed expression.   resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {       return(ptr_missing_expr())     }     rlang::expr(.log_safe(!!rlang::sym(value)))   },    # Bind the helper the generated call references by name.   prepare_eval_env = function(input, metas, eval_env, context) {     eval_env$.log_safe <- function(x) log(pmax(x, .Machine$double.eps))     eval_env   },    copy_defaults = list(label = "Log-transform column for {param}") ) registry <- ptr_merge_placeholders(list(log_num = log_num))  obj <- ptr_parse_formula(   "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = log_num)) + geom_point()",   placeholders = registry )  obj$formula_text #> [1] "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = log_num)) + geom_point()" runtime <- ptr_exec(   obj,   list(     "geom_point_checkbox" = TRUE,     "ggplot_3_3"          = "mpg"   ) )  runtime$code_text #> [1] "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = .log_safe(mpg))) +\n  geom_point()" ptr_app(   "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = log_num)) + geom_point()",   placeholders = registry )

title: Custom Placeholders with the ggpaintr Registry
text: custom keyword matches built-one wins — built-evicted merged registry. , replace var shinyWidgets::pickerInput() offers search tick-boxes keeping original reactive behaviour (columns populated data).
code: picker_var <- ptr_define_placeholder(   keyword = "var",    # Container filled in by bind_ui once data is known.   build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {     shiny::uiOutput(paste0(id, "_container"))   },    # Produce the same symbol the default `var` does.   resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || identical(value, "(none)")) {       return(ptr_missing_expr())     }     rlang::sym(value)   },    # Mirror the reactive-column behaviour of the default `var`.   bind_ui = function(input, output, metas, context) {     eval_env <- context$eval_env     ggplot_expr <- context$ptr_obj$expr_list[["ggplot"]]     data_expr <- if (is.call(ggplot_expr)) ggplot_expr$data else NULL     cols <- character(0)     if (!is.null(eval_env) && !is.null(data_expr)) {       d <- tryCatch(eval(data_expr, envir = eval_env), error = function(e) NULL)       if (is.data.frame(d)) {         cols <- names(d)       }     }     for (meta in metas) {       local({         m <- meta         output[[paste0(m$id, "_container")]] <- shiny::renderUI({           shinyWidgets::pickerInput(             inputId = m$id,             label   = paste("Column for", m$param),             choices = c("(none)", cols),             options = shinyWidgets::pickerOptions(liveSearch = TRUE)           )         })       })     }     invisible(NULL)   },    copy_defaults = list(     label      = "Column for {param}",     empty_text = "(none)"   ) )  replacement_registry <- ptr_merge_placeholders(list(var = picker_var)) identical(replacement_registry$var, picker_var) #> [1] TRUE ptr_app(   "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) + geom_point()",   placeholders = replacement_registry )

title: ggpaintr Workflow
text: Teaching ggplot, exploring new dataset, running quick demo usually means writing small Shiny app audience can click variables. app almost always boilerplate: sidebar selectInput()s, renderPlot(), harness rebuilds plot whenever inputs change. ggpaintr removes boilerplate. describe plot formula string looks like ordinary ggplot code, ggpaintr turns placeholder token string right Shiny input widget, evaluates completed call, renders plot generated code. workflow aimed three situations: Teaching — show changing one aesthetic (color = var) changes plot without writing UI code. Exploration — point app dataset try several aesthetic--column assignments committing one. Prototyping — stand interactive plot minutes share collaborators, later migrate generated ggplot call reproducible script report. vignette covers Level 1 ggpaintr: spinning one-call app. Levels 2 3 (embedding ggpaintr Shiny app, driving runtime directly without Shiny) covered vignette("ggpaintr-extensibility") vignette("ggpaintr-placeholder-registry").

what: 5. What happens when you click “Update plot”
title: ggpaintr Workflow
text: Knowing pipeline high level helps app misbehaves. full sequence : Two steps public functions can call directly — ptr_parse_formula() ptr_exec(). entry point Level 3 (driving runtime without Shiny) covered extensibility vignette. Level 1 usage need touch either one. tiny illustration first step: element obj$expr_list one ggplot layer. entry obj$keywords_list lists placeholder keywords appear inside corresponding layer. second step — inspecting inputs runtime expects: Columns returned data frame: input_id — Shiny input id. Treat opaque; hand-author. role — "placeholder" (widget value), "layer_checkbox" (layer /toggle), "upload_name" (name field accompanies upload placeholder). layer_name — ggplot layer input belongs ("ggplot", "geom_point", …). param_key — ggplot argument name ("x", "color", …); NA_character_ unnamed positional placeholders. keyword — placeholder type ("var", "num", …). source_id — upload_name rows, input_id matching upload placeholder row.

code: library(ggplot2)  obj <- ptr_parse_formula(   "ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) + geom_point()" ) spec <- ptr_runtime_input_spec(obj) inputs <- setNames(vector("list", nrow(spec)), spec$input_id) inputs[spec$role == "layer_checkbox"] <- rep(list(TRUE), sum(spec$role == "layer_checkbox")) runtime <- ptr_exec(obj, inputs) plot_obj <- ptr_assemble_plot(runtime$complete_expr_list, runtime$eval_env) inherits(plot_obj, "ggplot") #> [1] TRUE

title: Capture Out-of-Runtime ggplot Additions for the Code Output — ptr_gg_extra
text: helper intentionally exposed ptr_app() ptr_app_bslib(). meaningful caller owns renderPlot({...}) block built ptr_server_state() ptr_register_*() helpers. Semantics: Replace-per-call. call overwrites previously captured extras. Pass every component want single call, e.g. ptr_gg_extra(ps, theme_minimal(), scale_x_log10()). Suppression failure. underlying runtime reports ok = FALSE, code binder ignores extras stale values prior successful draw never surface failed draw. new S3 class. return value plain list, plot_obj + ptr_gg_extra(ps, ...) dispatches ggplot2's built-ggplot_add.list method.

Use public Shiny integration surface (ptr_server_state(), ptr_register_*(), ptr_input_ui(), ptr_output_ui(), ptr_app_bslib()) compose distribute custom apps. placeholder distribution parameters source_file, source_package, source_function, on_missing removed ptr_define_placeholder(). existed support exporter. Hook functions defined inline. last commit removals tagged v0-pre-export-removal.

title: ggpaintr 0.1.0
text: repositioned package around maintained ggpaintr workflow shortened API prefix: exported functions now use ptr_* prefix conciseness (previously ggpaintr_*) improved semantic clarity function names across public API (e.g., bind_* → register_*, *_value → extract_*) renamed copy_rules parameter/system ui_text throughout migrated error signaling base::stop() rlang::abort() added @examples exported functions clarified maintained public API boundary README pkgdown-facing docs beginner path stays centered wrapper, integration, export, placeholder, intentionally exported runtime helpers removed generated documentation topics package-internal helper functions internal implementation details longer presented part public community-facing surface updated ptr_generate_shiny() maintained public call path now ptr_generate_shiny(ptr_obj, output_file, ...) Note: ptr_generate_shiny() removed 0.9.0 — see breaking changes . archived legacy package implementation archive/legacy-package/ replaced legacy package docs, vignette, pkgdown content ggpaintr-first documentation added roxygen2-based package documentation active implementation prepared package structure R CMD check CRAN-oriented cleanup
